findings.title: Real-time Presence Indicators Dependency
findings.description: The implementation of real-time presence indicators is a prerequisite for enabling simultaneous editing. This feature must be developed before real-time co-editing can be fully realized.
assumptions: Presence indicators are essential for user awareness during collaboration.

evidence.excerpt: Requested feature: real-time presence avatars in the document header showing active viewers and editors.

recommendations: Prioritize development of presence indicators in the MVP.

findings.title: Conflict Resolution Strategy Risk
findings.description: The choice of conflict resolution strategy (OT vs CRDT) poses a risk as it impacts data integrity and user experience. A decision must be made early to avoid delays.
assumptions: The selected strategy will significantly affect the performance and reliability of the editing experience.

evidence.excerpt: As we move toward real-time co-editing, a formal conflict resolution strategy is required.

recommendations: Evaluate both strategies and finalize the approach in the MVP phase.

findings.title: Mobile Editing Support Requirement
findings.description: Mobile editing support must be included in the initial release to ensure parity with desktop and meet user expectations. This requires WebSocket support and a touch-friendly UI.
assumptions: Mobile users expect a seamless editing experience comparable to desktop.

evidence.excerpt: As we build real-time collaboration, mobile must be a first-class client from day one.

recommendations: Incorporate mobile editing support in the MVP.

findings.title: Document Locking Mechanism Update
findings.description: The current document locking mechanism must be updated to support real-time editing. This change is necessary to prevent user frustration and improve collaboration.
assumptions: Users need a more flexible document access model to enhance collaboration.

evidence.excerpt: CollabDocs currently implements pessimistic locking: when a user opens a document for editing, it is locked to that user.

recommendations: Revise the document locking mechanism as part of the MVP.

summary: The analysis identifies critical dependencies and risks associated with implementing real-time collaboration features in CollabDocs. Key components such as presence indicators, conflict resolution strategies, and document locking mechanisms must be prioritized in the MVP to ensure a competitive offering. Mobile support is also essential from the outset to meet user expectations.
